title: Business-area taxonomy file and cascading three-level picker
epicSource: f-review-ux · Phase 4 review UX improvements approved 2026-07-19
userStory: As a user reviewing my bootstrapped profile, I want to select my Business Area, Portfolio, and Squad from a structured cascading picker seeded with real org taxonomy values — and to add new entries that persist — so that my team context is structured data rather than free text.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The taxonomy file (per ADR-P6-010: committed, human-editable markdown at `context/org-structure.md`; readable by the Work HQ server at startup, not inside `docs/data/local/`, not gitignored) is committed with the following seed data: six business areas ('Residential', 'Commercial', 'New Homes and Media', 'Experience and Platforms', 'Financial Services', 'Product Operations'); each business area contains a portfolios list seeded with at least two entries (seed labels: 'Portfolio 1', 'Portfolio 2'); each portfolio contains a squads list seeded with at least three entries (seed labels: 'Squad name 1', 'Squad name 2', 'Squad name 3'). Structure per ADR-P6-010 / file-contracts §1.4: `## <Business Area>` headings, `- <Portfolio>` bullets, `  - <Squad>` two-space nested bullets, under a `# Org Structure · Updated: YYYY-MM-DD` header. After the story is shipped, `grep -rn 'Residential\|New Homes and Media\|Experience and Platforms\|Financial Services' context/org-structure.md` returns at least one match per business area name.

A server function `readTaxonomy` (a `createServerFn` GET, following the pattern of `work-hq/src/server/load-onboarding.ts`) reads and parses the taxonomy file, returning `{ businessAreas: Array<{ name: string; portfolios: Array<{ name: string; squads: string[] }> }> }`. A companion server function `writeTaxonomy` (or `saveTaxonomy`) accepts the same structure and writes it back to the taxonomy file, enabling user additions to persist. Both server files live under `work-hq/src/server/`. `grep -n 'readTaxonomy\|writeTaxonomy\|saveTaxonomy' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns at least one match per function name at the import or call site, and the corresponding server files exist.

The 'Business area' section in `PhaseReviewCorrect` renders a three-level cascading picker (routed from `selectRenderer` returning `'taxonomy-picker'` per story 019). Level 1 selects a Business Area from the taxonomy; Level 2 shows Portfolios for the selected Business Area — hidden or disabled when no Business Area is selected; Level 3 shows Squads for the selected Portfolio — hidden or disabled when no Portfolio is selected. Each level resets when its parent changes. The picker uses the `<Select>` or `<Popover>`+`<Command>` pattern already in use for `RoleCombobox` and `KeyToolsCombobox` in `onboarding.tsx`. `grep -n 'businessArea\|BusinessArea\|TaxonomyPicker\|taxonomy' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns at least three distinct matches.

The user can add a new entry at any level by typing a name not in the current list and confirming (Enter key or selecting an 'Add "<typed name>"…' option in the combobox, consistent with the pattern used for `KeyToolsCombobox`). New entries are written to the taxonomy file via `writeTaxonomy` immediately after confirmation. After a page reload, the new entry appears in its respective picker level. `grep -n 'writeTaxonomy\|saveTaxonomy' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns at least one match at a call site that fires on user confirmation of a new entry.

The chosen Business-Area → Portfolio → Squad triple is stored in `answers.editedSections` for the 'Business area' section as a single `Fact`: `{ id: 'business-area-selection', decision: 'edited', text: '', editedText: '<BusinessArea> > <Portfolio> > <Squad>' }`. This produces exactly one bullet in the profile-corrections handoff (file-contracts §4.3): `- <BusinessArea> > <Portfolio> > <Squad>` under the `**Business area**` section heading. If only a Business Area and Portfolio are selected (no Squad), `editedText` = `'<BusinessArea> > <Portfolio>'`. If only a Business Area is selected, `editedText` = `'<BusinessArea>'`. `cat docs/data/local/onboarding-state.json` after selecting all three levels shows the 'Business area' key under `editedSections` with a `facts` array containing one entry with `id: 'business-area-selection'` and the correct `editedText` value.

Each picker level carries an `aria-label` identifying its role: `aria-label='Business area'`, `aria-label='Portfolio'`, `aria-label='Squad'`. Level 2 and Level 3 carry `aria-disabled='true'` (or `disabled` on `<select>`) when their parent level has not been selected. All three levels are reachable by keyboard: Tab reaches each control; Enter or Space opens it; arrow keys navigate options. `grep -n "aria-label.*Business area\|aria-label.*Portfolio\|aria-label.*Squad" work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns at least one match per level.

technicalNotes: Depends on story 019: `selectRenderer` and `HEADING_OVERRIDE_MAP` must already map 'Business area' → `'taxonomy-picker'`. The taxonomy file format (JSON, YAML, TOML, or structured Markdown) and path are the architect's decision — the server function must parse whatever format the architect specifies, and the PM spec does not constrain it. Model `readTaxonomy` on `work-hq/src/server/load-onboarding.ts` (createServerFn GET, read file, return JSON). Model `writeTaxonomy` on `work-hq/src/server/save-onboarding.ts` (createServerFn mutation, accept JSON, write file). Load taxonomy in `PhaseReviewCorrect` via the route loader (extend the existing loader return object to include `taxonomy`) or via an initial `useEffect` server call — the architect's file-location decision may influence which is cleaner. For the three-level picker, the `Popover` + `Command` pattern (already present for `RoleCombobox`/`KeyToolsCombobox`) handles both selection and free-entry 'Add…' options. Level 2 and 3 state resets in a `useEffect` that watches the parent selection. The business-area `Fact` (AC5) is written to `editedSections` on every selection change via the existing `updateFact` / `set` debounced path. `buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown` handles the single fact via its standard `decision: 'edited'` / `editedText` path — no special-casing needed. Files likely touched: `work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx`, `work-hq/src/server/read-taxonomy.ts` (new), `work-hq/src/server/write-taxonomy.ts` (new), taxonomy file (new, path per architect).
outOfScope: KV-grid renderer (story 020). Item-list renderer (story 021). Section suppression in the handoff (story 023). Synchronising taxonomy entries with any external system (Jira, Confluence, etc.). Changing the profile-corrections handoff header line or the §4.3 Ask block. Any non-onboarding routes.

verificationPlan.command: grep -rn 'Residential\|New Homes and Media\|Experience and Platforms\|Financial Services' context/org-structure.md
verificationPlan.checks: Returns at least one match per business area name — confirming the seeded taxonomy file (ADR-P6-010: context/org-structure.md) is committed.
Returns at least one match per function name — confirming both the read and write server functions are imported and called from the route.
Returns at least one match per level — confirming all three picker controls carry descriptive aria-labels.

impl.notes: TaxonomyPickerCard uses Popover+Command pattern (KeyToolsCombobox) with three cascading levels. Taxonomy state loaded via useEffect in PhaseReviewCorrect. writeTaxonomy fires on each 'Add…' confirmation — full-file canonical re-serialisation. Selection persists as a single 'business-area-selection' Fact with editedText joined by ' > '. Business area counts in review/settled totals: 1 needing review until all 3 levels selected, 0 once settled. CONTROL_TRIGGER constant is defined after TaxonomyPickerCard in file order but is safe since function bodies execute at render time after all module-level const declarations are initialized.
verificationRun: grep -rn 'Residential|New Homes and Media|Experience and Platforms|Financial Services' context/org-structure.md → 4 matches PASS
grep -n 'readTaxonomy|writeTaxonomy|saveTaxonomy' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx → 7 matches (import + call sites) PASS
grep -n "aria-label.*Business area|aria-label.*Portfolio|aria-label.*Squad" work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx → 3 matches (one per level) PASS
node harness: parse 6×2×3 seeded file → PASS; round-trip identity → PASS; add-entry preserves existing → PASS; selection fact → correct handoff bullet (full/partial/BA-only) → PASS
npm run --prefix work-hq build → ✓ built in ~760ms client + ~507ms SSR PASS
